name: Italian Center Attack
description: An excellent opening for beginners and intermediates, relying on an early central attack. Pair with the Fried Liver course.

name: The Center Game
description: The Center Game is like the Scandinavian, but for White. We bring our queen out early and start a an attack. Perhaps a little too risky for beginners, although anyone can try!

name: Aggressive Italian Game
description: A powerful opening repertoire in 1.e4 e5, based on many central gambits such as the Max Lange, Deutz, and Double Knight. Better for intermediate and advanced players as there is more memorization, but available to all levels.

name: King's Knight Opening
description: Beat the Petrov, Philidor, and other lines. Pair this course with Italian, Scotch, Ponziani, or another opening.

name: Aggressive Ruy Lopez
description: The Ruy Lopez, or Spanish, is one of the most famous chess openings. In this course we play a quick d4 push.

name: d4 Sidelines For Black
description: Learn to play against the London, Trompowsky, and other White sidelines after d4 Nf6. Combine with another main course.

name: The Portuguese Gambit
description: This repertoire features both the Portuguese and Icelandic Gambits and is a great attacking weapon for Black.

name: The Stonewall Dutch
description: A reliable defense, focusing on building a brick wall of pawns on light squares. Features many attacking ideas, so good for more aggressive/tactical players rather than patient ones.

name: The Scandinavian Defense
description: This is one of Black's best openings against 1.e4 below the master level. We almost always get a pretty easy game. Levy wins nearly 70% of the time with the Scandinavian Defense.

name: Latvian Gambit
description: A risky gambit against White's most common opening moves. Best for intermediate and beyond as you weaken your king.

name: The Jaenisch Gambit
description: A variation against White playing the Spanish or Ruy Lopez. We strike at the center and immediately seize the initiative. Very fun positions leading to super complicated games. Could be a little dangerous for beginners, but try it out!

name: The Traxler Counterattack
description: One of the BEST openings for Black against White's very famous Fried Liver Attack. You will win many games in under 15 moves with massive attacks on your opponent's king.

name: The Danish Gambit
description: Hyper-aggressive and tricky TRIPLE pawn gambit. If you're below 1500, this could score you some very crushing victories. Otherwise, the positions are fun and full of attacks.

name: The Czech Benoni
description: The Czech Benoni is an easy-to-learn defense that can become your main weapon with Black against 1.d4. You simply lock up the position and launch a huge attack.

name: The Hillbilly Attack
description: Although silly in name, this opening has been played by none other than Magnus Carlsen. It's a creative attacking weapon against the Caro-Kann defense. Due to the length of variations, I recommend this above intermediate level.

name: The Englund Gambit
description: The famous, fun gambit against White's 1.d4 which can get you a checkmate in 8 moves. Obviously a bit more dubious as you rise up the rating ladder, but for beginners: epic!

name: The Grunfeld Defense
description: One of the best defenses against White's 3 major openings: 1.d4, 1.c4, and 1.Nf3. We have an easy to learn repertoire available for you to play like the Grandmasters.

name: The Smith-Morra Gambit
description: A nasty gambit repertoire against 1.e4 c5, the Sicilian Defense. We rely on a pawn sacrifice to build a massive attack against our opponent and deliver swift checkmate.

name: 1.e4 Gotham Style Part 3: Other Openings
description: In Part 3 of the e4 course, we address the Caro-Kann, French, Pirc, Modern, Philidor, Scandinavian, and other random defenses.

name: The Budapest Gambit
description: A fun counterpunch against the Queen's Gambit by White, and a bit more solid than the Albin. Also features a trap which can win you the game in less than 10 moves!

name: The Modern Defense
description: A tactical Black setup based on a kingside fianchetto which can be played against ALL of White's openings. Played by current GM Hikaru Nakamura and future GM Levy Rozman.

name: The London System
description: One of the most famous openings in chess, and for good reason. It is very easy to learn, and you can choose between slower paced games or vicious attacks on your opponent.

name: The Gotham Dutch
description: The easiest Dutch Defense to learn, based on an early trade and a very straightforward setup of pieces. Harder to play above 2000 elo, but before that - fantastic!

name: The Bird Opening
description: Take flight with this amazing offbeat weapon. The Bird creates a central blockade with simple plans, often turning into huge attacks onto our opponent. Recommended for intermediate and advanced Chessly users.

name: The Gotham Gambit
description: A tricky choice against 1.e4, this Petroff Gambit leads to attacking positions where you can crush your opponent.

name: Anti-Sicilians For Black
description: A supplementary course to learning the Sicilian Defense. Learn to beat the Alapin, Smith-Morra, Rossolimo, and more.

name: The Beefeater Defense
description: A tricky system against d4, c4, and Nc3, which tries to take your opponents out of their comfort zone into murky positions.

name: Queen's Gambit Declined
description: The "QGD" is a solid repertoire against 3/4 major White openings: d4, c4, and Nf3. This repertoire is based on 3...a6, the Janowski Variation.

name: The Exchange Caro-Kann
description: A simple yet effective solution against the Caro-Kann is the exchange variation. We trade on d5 and develop our pieces. This variation is played from beginner to master level.

name: Four Knights Sicilian
description: A dynamic and fun Sicilian variation. Pair this course with an Anti-Sicilian repertoire to be ready in e4 c5.

name: The b3 French
description: Fight the French with the Papa-Ticulat Gambit. This opening pairs best with the b3 Sicilian.

name: The Sicilian Dragon
description: An easy-to-learn and easy-to-play variation of the legendary Sicilian Defense, relying on Black's dark squared bishop on g7.

name: 1.e4 Gotham Style Part 1: The Vienna
description: In Part 1, we focus on 1.e4 e5 by Black, and Levy's main recommendation: the wonderful Vienna. Pair this course with the "Dark Vienna" to really skyrocket your Vienna power.

name: The Trompowsky Attack
description: This opening helped Levy reach 2200 elo. The goal is to prevent your opponents from playing many theoretical defenses, instead fighting on your terms.

name: Queen's Gambit Accepted
description: One of Levy's favorite openings, the "QGA" is an easy opening that leads to great positions for Black below master level. Simply capture White's c4 pawn and let the fun begin.

name: The French Defense
description: A building-block opening against White's center. Not great for beginners due to lack of space. Pair with Winawer etc.

name: Owen's Defense (e6 b6)
description: A setup which is possible against EVERY White opening. Quite tricky and often results in winning a central pawn early.

name: The English Opening
description: One of the "Big 3" chess openings is now on Chessly. The English is one of Levy's favorites, and an opening you can play for your entire chess career. Best suited for intermediate and advanced players, but of course anyone can try!

name: The Alapin Sicilian
description: An easy setup against the Sicilian Defense. Played even at Grandmaster level, but especially effective under 1800 elo.

name: The Albin Countergambit
description: A tricky response to the Queen's Gambit by White based on the central strike e5!?, leading to very fun positions. Sometimes, your opponent will lose in just 8 moves!

name: Larsen's Opening: 1.b3
description: A fun, offbeat opening best suited for players who like crazy positions. Better beyond the beginner level.

name: The Fried Liver Attack
description: This course teaches you the famous Fried Liver Attack out of the Italian Game. And as a bonus, you learn how to play a regular aggressive Italian after 3...Bc5. A great repertoire for beginners and intermediate players.

name: 1.e4 Gotham Style Part 2: The Sicilian
description: In Part 2 of the e4 course, the focus is on the Sicilian Defense. Levy's main recommendation is 2.a3, the Mengarini. Of course, we have many other weapons vs the Sicilian Defense here on Chessly as well.

name: Alekhine's Defense
description: A wild approach to White's most popular move, 1.e4. This opening will surprise your opponent and provoke them into a massive battle. A bit tricky to handle for beginners due to the nature of the positions, but great beyond that.

name: The Ponziani
description: This is the "Best Beginner Chess Opening" and for good reason. The plans are easy to learn and there are so many traps for Black to lose in 10 moves.

name: The Caro-Kann Defense
description: The opening that made Levy famous. An absolutely fantastic defense for Black which you can play up the entire rating ladder. Pair this opening with Levy's Caro-Kann playlist on YouTube, as well as the "bonus" course here on Chessly.

name: Anti-Sicilian For White
description: A sneaky variation against the Najdorf and Dragon Sicilians (2...d6). We play 3.Nc3 and trick Black into our system with a central strike.

name: The Leningrad Dutch
description: A dynamic defense based on Black's 1...f5 advance, which can be played against d4, c4, and Nf3 by White. Played by many strong Grandmasters, and hundreds of times by Levy.

name: The Scotch Game
description: The Scotch is one of the oldest openings in chess, dating back to the year 1750. It also was Levy's first ever chess opening! It's easy to learn and the ideas are quite fun.

name: Kalashnikov Sicilian
description: Levy's favorite Sicilian, the cousin of the Sveshnikov. Pair with an "Anti Sicilian" course to finish your repertoire.

name: The Caro-Kann Bonus Lines
description: In this bonus course, you receive several more suggested variations for your repertoire, so that you can choose specific lines to play in your Caro-Kann!

name: King's Indian Defense
description: The famous system opening for Black which can be played against every single White opening besides e4.

name: The b3 Sicilian
description: A powerful offbeat weapon against the Sicilian Defense, ideal to pair with the b3 French.

name: The Dark Vienna
description: Take your Vienna knowledge to the next level with this BONUS mini course. This pairs well with the 1.e4 Gotham Style Part 1.

name: Dutch Defense Sidelines
description: A mini course focusing on White's aggressive sidelines in the Dutch Defense, like the Staunton Gambit and more. This course should be paired with any other Chessly Dutch to complete your repertoire.
